titel:
Stricter rules for young offenders.

# Skärpta regler för unga lagöverträdare

Nya åtgärder har införts för att hantera ungdomar som begår brott.

*   Syftet är att minska brottsligheten bland unga.
*   Åtgärderna inkluderar:
    *   Ökad övervakning.
    *   Striktare straff.
    *   Fler möjligheter till rehabilitering.

Dessa åtgärder är tänkta att ge unga som begår brott en andra chans och att hjälpa dem att återintegreras i samhället.

**Viktigt att notera:** Det är viktigt att komma ihåg att dessa åtgärder är till för att hjälpa unga, inte att straffa dem.

För mer information, besök: www.exempel.se
html:
Systematic and organized crime has increasingly affected more and more aspects of society in recent years. Serious crime among young people is particularly concerning and is occurring at increasingly younger ages. To ensure that legislation meets the needs arising from this trend in crime, and to maintain the credibility and legitimacy of the criminal justice system, the rules for young offenders need to be tightened. Therefore, the government proposes, among other things, the following:

  • The age of criminal responsibility will be lowered to 13 years for serious crimes, for a period of five years.
  • The youth discount will be completely abolished for young adults.
  • The youth discount will be reduced, and the maximum sentence will be increased to 18 years of imprisonment for offenders under 18 years of age.
  • The supervision measure for young offenders will be strengthened.
  • The regulations regarding misconduct during the probationary measures of youth care and community service will be tightened.

These legislative changes are proposed to come into effect on August 2, 2026, in all essential respects.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Prop. 2025/26:246

If a person has committed a crime before reaching the age of eighteen, their youth must be taken into account to a reasonable extent when determining the sentence. In this assessment, consideration should be given to the offender's age at the time of the crime and the type of crime. The court may then impose a less severe sentence.
tldr: The government proposes stricter penalties for young people who commit crimes. The proposal includes, among other things, that children as young as 13 years old can be held responsible for serious crimes for a period of five years.
lattlast:
The government wants to change the laws to curb the increasing crime among young people. One of the most important proposals is to lower the age at which someone can be punished for serious crimes to 13 years old. This proposal would be in effect for a period of five years.

For young adults who are of legal age but have committed crimes, the government wants to abolish the so-called youth discount. This means that the penalties should not be reduced simply because the person is young. For individuals under 18 years old, it is also proposed to increase the maximum possible sentences to 18 years in prison.

Other types of penalties and controls will also become stricter. This includes tightening the rules for youth supervision and being stricter if young people do not comply with their community service or youth care requirements. The aim is for the rules to be more effective against organized crime.

If these changes are approved by the parliament, the government plans for the new rules to come into effect on August 2, 2026. The proposal also includes changes to many other laws, such as laws regarding taxes, police work, and how personal data is handled.
